id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0000
text: # Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0004

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0004
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0005
text: Since 2018, investors have put 793 million dollars into young companies that apply “generative AI” for sustainability purposes. This form of artificial intelligence can create many things based on instructions, such as text, 3D designs and even new materials. However, the potential sustainability gains come with the high energy use of AI technology.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0005
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0013

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0013
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0014
text: Developments in artificial intelligence, also called AI for short, accelerated after the ultra-smart chatbot ChatGPT was introduced at the end of 2022. Since then, the big technology companies have raced to introduce new AI models that generate ever better answers to increasingly complex questions and, based on written instructions, create impressive images and video material.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0014
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0015
text: Although people often talk about the entertainment value of generative AI and potential productivity gains, there are also potential applications in sustainability. We cannot ignore this perspective now that the effects of global warming are painfully clear in many places. The new technology can complement legislation and regulation, financing and behavioural change.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0015
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0016
text: ## Twin Transition: where technology and sustainability meet

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0016
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0017
text: The rise of generative AI is a textbook example of the “twin transition”. This term refers to the digital transition and the sustainability transition, which take place at the same time and influence each other. Companies that are further ahead in digitalisation perform, for example, [better](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0040162522007934) on sustainability. Scientific literature also points to a positive contribution by AI to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) defined by the United Nations. Specifically for the SDGs related to climate change, natural ecosystems and making sectors more sustainable, this applies to 50 of the 55 sub-targets. At the same time, AI also appears to have a dampening effect on 18 of these sub-targets — sometimes at the same time. This is partly due to the large amount of energy needed to develop and use AI models.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0017
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0018
text: Investors see value in AI as a supporter of the sustainability transition. In 2021 — when the technology market showed huge growth after the initial “coronavirus shock” — investors worldwide put 25.2 billion dollars into sustainable AI start-ups, according to data from data collector Net Zero Insights. In 2022, this amount fell to 21.4 billion dollars due to rising interest rates and a generally less favourable investment climate, and 2023 has not been immune to these dynamics either. On 1 December, the counter stood at 18.5 billion.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0018
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0019
text: This report explores various sustainable applications of generative AI based on investments in start-ups and scale-ups that are active in this area. These applications are already firmly embraced in the field of corporate reporting and analysis of climate risk and climate impact. Several software companies use AI’s language talent to convert large amounts of company data into clear reports and insights. Virtual assistants are also prominent. They answer questions about sustainability issues in a natural dialogue and can provide advice to the user based on all kinds of data. Together, these activities account for 258 million dollars in investments since 2018.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0019
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0020
text: In addition, generative AI can be used to design new materials and proteins more quickly, with specific applications and benefits. Examples include green fuels, materials optimised for lifespan and recyclability, or enzymes that can break down plastic. Research into such new molecules is traditionally time-consuming with much trial and error. Generative AI can greatly shorten this. Investors have so far put 85 million dollars into young companies in this category.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0020
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0021
text: A more nascent function of generative AI is that of designer or engineer. The technology can, for example, help to personalise clothing so that the pieces produced match market demand better, and the negative environmental impact of unsold — and therefore needlessly produced — clothing is reduced. There are also opportunities in the built environment, such as buildings that are optimised with AI for energy efficiency and material use. Current design methods are expected to be increasingly enhanced with generative AI.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0021
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0022
text: Developing generative AI also has a price. The technology works on the basis of large amounts of data, and the associated learning process — also called “training” — requires a lot of computing power. Training GPT-3.0 — one of the predecessors of the current models behind ChatGPT — was accompanied by 502 tonnes of CO2 emissions; the same as the annual emissions of 251 Dutch petrol cars. The constant stream of questions the model has to process also requires considerable computing power and energy. Although the applications outlined above are not used on the same scale as the immensely popular chatbot, it remains important to weigh both the positive and negative effects carefully when developing and applying generative AI.

metadata.chunk_id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0022
metadata.source_slug: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie
metadata.source_title: Generative AI enters the sustainability transition

id: Generatieve AI pakt rol in de duurzaamheidstransitie__a2-late-chunking__0027
text: Technology, Media & Telecom (TMT) is one of the fastest-growing sectors. Helped by the increasing use of software, algorithms and sensors, this sector is at the heart of the digital renewal of other sectors.
